item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents consistently oppose the statement from their first turn through their final turn. Agent A repeatedly argues that society matters but cannot be a blank check over rights; Agent B uses a similar U.S. civil-liberties frame and ends slightly more strongly worded. Both concede narrow legitimate interests in safety, health, order, or public harmony, but neither visibly changes position or flips.
